rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL ELY ET AL VS. UNITED SERVICES AUTOMOBILE ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 8, 2025, Line 1, DEFENDANT UNITED SERVICES AUTOMOBILE ASSOCIATION MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE. Grant, no opposition filed. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: DEMAURIAE VAUGHN VS. THE BOARD OF TRUSTEES OF THE CALIFORNIA STATE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories (General) Propounded On Defendant Karen Rubin And Request For Sanctions; And (2) Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for Tuesday, April 8, 2025, Line 8, 5. Plaintiff Vaughn's motion to compel further responses to Form Interrogatories propounded against Defendant Rubin is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. 1. The motion is GRANTED as to Form Interrogatory 2.1, 2.6, 2.11, 12.2, 15.1. The objections to these requests are meritless as the interrogatories are approved by the judicial council. 2. To the extent Defendant Rubin maintains her objections to Form Interrogatory 12.2, 15.1 based on privilege, she is required to produce a code-compliant privilege log within 10 days of this order. 3. The motion is DENIED as to Form Interrogatory 17.1. Defendant Rubin's responses were sufficient. 4. Defendant did not act with substantial justification. The court awards $2,000 in sanctions payable by Defendant and her attorneys to Plaintiff's attorneys within 20 days of the date of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: DEMAURIAE VAUGHN VS. THE BOARD OF TRUSTEES OF THE CALIFORNIA STATE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories (General And Employment) Propounded On Defendant The Board Of Trustees Of The California State University And Request For Sanctions; And (2) Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for Tuesday, April 8, 2025, Line 8, As to all motions the judge orders meet and confer prior to the hearing. 1. PLAINTIFF DEMAURIAE VAUGHN'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories (General And Employment) Propounded On Defendant The Board Of Trustees Of The California State University And Request For Sanctions. Plaintiff Vaughn's Motion to compel further responses to Form Interrogatories propounded against Defendant Board of Trustees is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. 1. The motion is GRANTED as to Form Interrogatories: No. 12.3, 17.1, 200.4, 207. 2, 208.1, and 209.2. To the extent Defendant maintains its objections based on privilege, it must produce a privilege log within 10 days of this order. 2. The motion is DENIED as to Form Interrogatory 15.1. 3. To the extent Defendant maintains objections based on privilege, it must produce a code-compliant privilege log within 10 days of the date of this order. 4. No sanctions are warranted because both parties acted with substantial justification. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: DEMAURIAE VAUGHN VS. THE BOARD OF TRUSTEES OF THE CALIFORNIA STATE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories Propounded On Defendant The Board Of Trustees Of The California State University And Request For Sanctions; And (2) Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for Tuesday, April 8, 2025, Line 8, 2. PLAINTIFF DEMAURIAE VAUGHN'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories Propounded On Defendant The Board Of Trustees Of The California State University And Request For Sanctions. Plaintiff Vaughn's motion to compel further responses to Special Interrogatories propounded against Defendant Board of Trustees is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. 1. The motion is GRANTED as to Special Interrogatories 11, 12, 14, 15, 23, 25, 26, and 29. The students' last known contact information is discoverable and Plaintiff's need for the information outweighs the rights to privacy under FERPA. 2. The motion is GRANTED as to Special Interrogatories 37, 38, 39, 63, 64, 65, 66, 69, 81, 82, 83, 84, 87, 99, 100, 101, and 105. The reference to the document production as a whole is insufficient and sanctionable. 3. The motion is DENIED as to all other Special Interrogatories. 4. To the extent Defendant maintains objections based on privilege, it must produce a code-compliant privilege log within 10 days of the date of this order. 5. Defendant did not act with substantial justification. The court awards $1,000 in sanctions payable by Defendant and its attorneys to Plaintiff's attorneys within 20 days of the date of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/REQ)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents Propounded On Defendant The Board Of Trustees Of The California State University And Request For Sanctions; And (2) Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for Tuesday, April 8, 2025, Line 8, 3. PLAINTIFF DEMAURIAE VAUGHN'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents Propounded On Defendant The Board Of Trustees Of The California State University And Request For Sanctions. Plaintiff Vaughn's motion to compel further responses to Requests for Production propounded against Defendant Board of Trustees is GRANTED. 1. The motion is GRANTED as to RFPs 9, 13, 15, 19-23, 25-29, 31-32, and 41. Defendant has 30 days to produce the documents it promised to. 2. The motion is also GRANTED as to RFPs 4-6, 17, 33, 34, 35, and 40. Plaintiff's need for the information outweighs Defendant Rubin's privacy rights. 3. To the extent Defendant maintains objections based on privilege, it must produce a code-compliant privilege log within 10 days of the date of this order. 4. No sanctions are warranted because both parties acted with substantial justification. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: MAYU IZUMIDA ET AL VS. GAETANI REAL ESTATE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery Requests; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for Tuesday, April 8, 2025, Line 6, PLAINTIFFS MAYU IZUMIDA, JOSHUA STOLL'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery Requests; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant. Plaintiffs Maya Izumida and Joshua Stoll appear to concede in their Reply that co-Defendant Defendant Gaetani Real Estate, LLC, provided to Plaintiffs the information they seek from co-Defendant Nancy Pao-Jen, by way of Supplemental Response to Special Interrogatories, Set One, Nos. 11 and Nos. 12. Therefore, the motion is denied as moot. Plaintiff sending one email attempting to meet and confer before filing the motion to compel, is insufficient to demonstrate a reasonable and good faith attempt at an informal resolution. Plaintiffs' request for sanctions is denied. Defendant's request for sanctions raised in her belated Opposition is denied. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/REQ)

The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/REQ)
